product_overview: A backend API that ingests creator content samples and uses a CrewAI multi-agent pipeline to produce structured AI skill packages (SKILL.md + reference files), with preview and export capabilities.
core_goals: Automatically extract repeatable workflows and patterns from creator content and produce a complete SKILL.md skill package.
Return generated files in API responses for immediate preview and optionally persist packages to disk with downloadable zip archives.
Provide robust input validation and clear error responses (422 for invalid input, 404/400 for export errors, 500 for server errors).
Expose lightweight operational endpoints (health check, root info) for monitoring and discoverability.

features.name: Skill Generation API
features.description: Accept raw creator content and metadata, run the CrewAI pipeline (Analyst -> Writer) to generate a set of skill files (SKILL.md and references) and optionally persist/zip them.
user_flows: POST /api/generate-skill with body {creator_content: "long text >= 40 chars", content_kind: "generic", include_zip: true, persist_to_disk: true} -> Receive 200 with {skill_name, output_path, zip_path, files: [{relative_path, content}, ...], warnings: []} -> GET /api/skills -> Receive 200 with skills array including the generated skill and files metadata
POST /api/generate-skill with body {creator_content: "short text < 40 chars"} -> Receive 422 Validation error (creator_content too short)

POST /api/generate-skill with body {creator_content: "valid >= 40 chars", include_zip: false, persist_to_disk: false} -> Receive 200 with {skill_name, output_path: null, zip_path: null, files: [...], warnings: []}
POST /api/generate-skill with valid body but internal CrewAI failure (simulated) -> Receive 500 Internal server error with error detail

features.name: Export and Download
features.description: List generated skills, return raw file contents for clipboard copy, and serve individual files or a full zip archive for download.
user_flows: GET /api/skills -> Receive 200 with skills array (each skill: name, has_skill_md, has_zip, files[])
GET /api/export/{skill_name}/zip with existing skill_name -> Receive 200 application/zip file download (zip stream)
GET /api/export/{skill_name}/{file_path} with existing skill_name and safe file_path (e.g., SKILL.md) -> Receive 200 text/markdown or text/plain with file contents
POST /api/export/copy with body {skill_name: "existing", file_path: "references/examples.md"} -> Receive 200 with {content: "...file text...", file_path: "references/examples.md"}
GET /api/export/{skill_name}/zip with non-existent skill_name -> Receive 404 Zip not found for given skill name
GET /api/export/{skill_name}/{file_path} with path traversal attempt (file_path: "../secrets") -> Receive 400 Invalid path (path traversal attempt)
POST /api/export/copy with body {skill_name: "existing", file_path: "nonexistent.md"} -> Receive 404 File not found

features.name: Health Check
features.description: A lightweight endpoint that reports service health for monitoring and uptime checks.
user_flows: GET /api/health while service misconfigured or erroring -> Receive 500 Internal server error with diagnostic message

features.name: Root Info
features.description: Root endpoint providing usage information and directing clients to the generate-skill endpoint.
user_flows: GET / -> Receive 200 with {message: "Usage: POST /api/generate-skill with creator_content (min 40 chars)"}
GET / when backend has an internal fault -> Receive 500 Internal server error with error detail

known_limitations.issue: No rate limiting on any endpoint
known_limitations.impact: API can be overwhelmed by concurrent requests
Anyone can generate skills and consume API resources
Clients need long timeouts; no async processing available
